entry.title: Resolving Carrier Mismatch Errors When planning Freight Units within SAP Transportation Management
entry.content:
Transportation is a complex process, often requiring careful coordination of Transportation planners and various supply chain partners. This may require the Transportation planners to replan / reassign the demand (Freight Units) to the capacity (Freight order / Trucks). At times, one issue that can arise is a carrier mismatch error when trying to reassign Freight Units (FUs) to Freight Orders (FOs) after they have been unplanned. In this blog, we will explore this issue and offer some possible options to avoid this issue.

Let’s first look at the details of this issue.

When Freight Units are unplanned from an existing Freight Order, they become visible as unplanned in the SAP Transportation Management cockpit. However, attempting to reschedule them into a different freight order can result (under certain conditions) in a carrier mismatch error. This issue arises when the carrier for the freight units is "derived from predecessor documents," creating a discrepancy if the new Freight Order has a different carrier.

Here is a typical process that leads to this error:

1. Create a Sales order and a Freight Unit (FU) and, using the transportation cockpit, create a Road Freight Order (FO) based on the FU.
2. Open the FO and assign a carrier to it. Save your changes.
3. Delete the FO using the appropriate option within the Transportation Cockpit.
4. Next, create a new, empty FO with a different carrier and attempt to assign the initial FU to the newly created FO by selecting both and clicking "Assign Selected Items.”
5. Save the assignment process and encounter no error at this point.

However, the process can also take place when you have a Delivery created. Here the Freight unit is planned on the FO and the carrier may be assigned either to the delivery or to the Freight Order. Now, when the associated FU is unplanned from the FO, the carrier information remains attached to both the FU and the delivery. As you can see in the below screenshot it mentions “Derived from Predecessor Documents”.

At this point if an assignment is planned to a new FO with a different carrier it leads to the system giving an error “Freight unit xxxx cannot be assigned to Freight Order yyyy, carrier doesn't match”.

The system's retention of the carrier information can be due to various scenarios, such as the carrier being inherent in the delivery and thus needing to be retained despite the unplanning of Freight Order and Freight Unit. There can also be a scenario where the transportation planner may only be changing the Freight Order / Truck and continuing with the existing carrier.

To overcome this challenge, one might consider the following strategies

  1. When Carrier was assigned within Freight order
    1. Remove the Carrier from FO1 Before Unplanning / Deletion: By clearing the carrier assignment before unplanning / deleting the FO, you ensure that the FU / delivery has no carrier association and can be reassigned freely.
    2. Assign Carrier After Planning: Once you have unplanned the FU and need to reassign it, place it on a FO without a carrier and then assign the carrier to FO post-planning.
  2. When Carrier was assigned within Delivery
    1.  Delete Carrier from Delivery: Ensure that after FO Unplanning / deletion, the FU and delivery have no carrier specified. This allows reassignment to another FO without issues.

While carrier mismatch is a standard behavior that can occur due to multiple scenarios (some of which are mentioned above), understanding the underlying system logic and utilizing these strategies can facilitate smoother freight unit planning and assignment processes. Kindly note that there can be other options also that can be explored.

Should these solutions not address all nuances of your case, consider submitting a feature request on the [SAP Customer Influence Portal] (https://me.sap.com/notes/0002963059).

entry.title: Change Process in CPQ – BRIM S/4 Solution Quotation Integration
entry.content:
Change is the only constant thing is a common knowledge, so how can business documents be an exception to this? It is not! We live in constantly evolving dynamic times, where business strategies, negotiations and deals are also being adapted and changed based on multitude of influencing factors. So, with every change, how the change is managed and how we execute and plan for change especially when it comes to contractual documents like Subscription contract is the topic for this blog.

The CPQ – BRIM integration enables SAP customers to have the best of on cloud CPQ for quoting process and on-prem BRIM for follow-on contract document creation to further proceed with the quote to cash scenario for revenue generation.  As part of contract lifecycle management, for subscription products, this process further extends to support changes to existing subscription contracts. These changes can be made to existing contracts or new contracts can be created for the changes triggered from an existing contract.

Changes like contract extension, renewal, cancellation, change in quantity/ price/configuration adding new products, swapping existing product with new products etc. are possible. Only specific change processes are available based on the state of the contract, e.g. cancelled contract cannot be extended or renewed.

Apart from BRIM prerequisites and configurations,  administrators need to enable access rights for the Assets Table Management and Change Processes sections as explained in Assign Access Rights for Setup Sections and Entities , else the sections won’t be visible in the Setup.

How subscription contract change-process works?

The term Change Process here refers to a singular change that can be executed on a subscription contract document. These Change Processes when group together based on business need and flexibility required form a group of processes called Change Process Groups. A single Change Process can belong to multiple change process groups. Example we have change process groups like Change Quantity and Price, where Change Quantity and Change Price are grouped together. Change Process Group column is synced from S/4 HANA, it can be renamed as per required. This column is reflected in the Assets section of the quote page. Kindly note not all change process in S/4 HANA are supported in this integration. Depending on the change process/group selected in the Asset Section, the subsequent fields in the product section become editable. The editability of the fields can be controlled from the Set-up section. From the above example, the quantity and price can be set to editable.

 

Special change process features like New Contract, Co-Term and Swap, must be additionally set for specific change process groups to further control the and align the behaviour to organization needs. New contract enables the sales rep to either create a new contract from the existing contracts or merge all contracts into one. On placing the order, the new contract is created in SAP S/4 HANA. The old contracts are cancelled, and the references are available in SAP S/4 HANA subscription contract document.

 

To change a particular Subscription contract or to be more specific the items of a subscription contract, we must add it to the quote via the Add Assets “+” icon. These items are termed as Assets and added to the Asset section of the quote. This Asset section can also be personalised via the set-up. The subscription contracts tied to the selected Involved Party in Quotes page are available to be added to quote as Assets.

 

 

 

After adding Assets to the quote page, if the toggle in the quote header section is turned ON to create a new subscription contract, the subsequent changes to the assets selected from one or more contracts will be transferred to a new contract starting on the day of initiating the changes and the existing contracts will be discontinued.

Mostly every change process has direct impact price or value of the quote. Contract Change Activation Date is the reference taken for adjusting all quote relevant price calculations so that prices are always in line with the changes and applicable from the date changes are implemented.

To conclude, for subscription products the SAP CPQ – BRIM integration offers great flexibility to change the already created subscription contracts with feasibility to group multiple changes, change multiple contracts at the same time create new contracts, merge contracts, cancel contracts etc. to enabling smooth management of the contract lifecycle.

Summary Settings for change process

SAP CPQ Set-UpSAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration/iflows)Solution Quotation in S/4 HANA BRIM
Quote Layout : Add Asset SectionRead Change Process Groups for Quote from SAP BRIMSubscription Contracts
Quotes -Assets Table Management : Configure the view of Asset TableRead Change Process for Quote from SAP BRIMChange Processes
Workflow: Preparing – Preparing: Action – Add Asset to Quote and other actionsRead allowed Change Process Groups for Subscription Contract Item from SAP BRIMChange Process Group
Quotes- Change Processes – Add Change Process, group and give relevant names to the group. Additional settings can be maintainedRead Subscription contract header from BRIM 
 Read Subscription contract items from BRIM 
 Replicate Change Contract Processing Status from SAP S4HANA 

 

Most used Change Process Capabilities

Sno

 Change Processes/ Capabilities

Description

Additional Info

1

New Contract

Enables to renew or change the existing subscription contract and create a new contract with reference to it.

On placing the order, the new/merged contract is created in SAP S/4 HANA. The old contracts are cancelled, and the references are available in SAP S/4 HANA subscription contract document.

2

Merge

Enables consolidation of multiple contracts into one contract.

3

Product Change/Swap

Enables the change/replacement of an existing subscription product in a contract.

In Set up -Quote -Change Processes -Change Process Group Tab, for the selected change process group, you must select the values for additional settings to Swap.

4

Co-Term

Enables user to co-term – adjust to the same contract duration for different items in a contact.

 

5

Cancellation

Enables users to cancel a contract item.

Cancellation process needs to be set-up.

6

Contract Extension

Enables the extension of the contact term as set-up in product master data).

 

7

Change Item Quantity

Enables users to change the item quantity for products in a contract.

 

8

Change Configuration 

Enables users to modify the variant configuration-based characteristics for the configurable products in a contract.

 

9

Change Price

Enables users to change the price of the products in the contract

 

10

Add Sub-item

Enables the addition of sub-items during change processes

 

 

Disclaimer: Given the dynamic nature of technology and features, and the potential limits to the author's knowledge, please consider the contents of this blog as an attempt to simplify the subject matter and disseminate further information about it. Therefore, it is recommended to verify the details from official sources or conduct further research to ensure complete accuracy and up-to-date information. The insights provided are meant to foster learning and discussion, not to serve as definitive guidance.

entry.title: Building a Hybrid Landscape: Decentralized SAP EWM with S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Overview
Integrating SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) with SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition) brings advanced warehouse operations and core ERP processes into real-time alignment. In a decentralized deployment—where EWM runs on a separate system—interfaces must be configured for master data replication and transaction exchange so that inventory movements, deliveries and warehouse tasks remain in sync with S/4HANA Cloud. SAP fully supports this scenario using modern, cloud-friendly integration tools. This guide presents the available integration approaches, an ideal architecture, step-by-step instructions and best practices, including security considerations and prerequisites.

Supported Integration Methods & Technologies

  1. SAP BTP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration)
    • Serves as the central iPaaS for connecting S/4HANA Cloud to external systems.
    • Provides pre‑built connectors, mapping tools and standard integration packages to accelerate setup.
  2. Whitelisted S/4HANA Cloud APIs (OData, SOAP)
    • Only RESTful OData and SOAP services (including SOAP‑wrapped IDoc structures) are available for integration.
    • Standard APIs cover delivery creation, goods movement confirmation and other warehouse processes, enabling synchronous, real‑time communication between systems.
  3. IDoc Message Exchange via Middleware
    • Traditional ALE/IDoc formats (e.g., MATMAS, DEBMAS) are sent through the Integration Suite rather than direct RFC.
    • Provides near‑real‑time replication of master data and transactional messages.
  4. Event‑Driven Integration with SAP Event Mesh
    • S/4HANA Cloud publishes business events (e.g., "Outbound Delivery Created") to Event Mesh.
    • An event‑based approach triggers integration flows instantly, reducing polling and enabling decoupled, asynchronous updates.

Recommended Architecture & Components

  • Hub‑and‑Spoke Model: SAP Integration Suite acts as the central hub, routing data between S/4HANA Cloud and EWM.
  • Communication Arrangements: Defined in S/4HANA Cloud to specify which APIs or events are used and the target endpoints on BTP.
  • Adapters & Cloud Connector: Use HTTP(S)/SOAP adapters for S/4 calls and IDoc/RFC adapters for EWM, secured via the SAP Cloud Connector when EWM resides on-premises.
  • Pre‑Built Content: Leverage SAP’s integration packages from the API Business Hub or Best Practice Explorer where available.
  • Secure Connectivity: All communication over encrypted HTTPS, with OAuth or certificate‑based authentication and minimal firewall exposure.

Integration Process – High-Level Steps

  1. Activate Scope & Prepare Systems
    • Confirm relevant EWM integration scope items are included in the S/4HANA Cloud project (e.g., outbound, inbound, STO, physical inventory).
    • Ensure EWM runs in decentralized mode on a compatible release and basic warehouse structure is configured.
  2. Set Up Connectivity & Middleware
    • Install and configure SAP Cloud Connector for secure access to on‑prem EWM.
    • Provision Integration Suite tenant, create destinations, import or build iFlows for each scenario.
    • Configure Communication Systems and Arrangements in S/4HANA Cloud for each API or event.
  3. Master Data Replication
    • Synchronize materials, batches and business partners via IDoc or OData APIs through the Integration Suite.
    • Perform initial data loads to ensure EWM has up‑to‑date master records before transactions flow.
  4. Transactional Integration
    • Outbound: Sales order → outbound delivery → delivery distribution → EWM picks/ships → goods issue confirmation back to ERP.
    • Inbound: Purchase order → inbound delivery → EWM receives/putaway → goods receipt confirmation to ERP.
    • STO & Production: Similar flows for stock transport orders and production staging using delivery documents.
    • Physical Inventory: Bin‑level counts in EWM posting adjustments back to S/4 inventory.
  5. Monitoring & Error Handling
    • Use SAP Integration Suite monitoring plus AIF/WE05 in EWM and message monitoring in S/4HANA Cloud.
    • Configure retries, alerts and duplicate detection to ensure robust, idempotent processing.

Best Practices & Security

  • Use SAP Standard Content: Start with delivered APIs and integration packages to minimize custom code and ensure supportability.
  • Real‑Time Triggers: Leverage event or change‑based triggers instead of batch replication for critical transactions.
  • Data Governance: Enforce a policy that all master data originates in S/4HANA Cloud and is replicated to EWM before use.
  • Authentication & Network Security: Employ OAuth 2.0 or certificates, restrict Cloud Connector paths and encrypt all traffic.
  • Performance Considerations: Balance synchronous calls and asynchronous flows to optimize throughput while maintaining near real‑time sync.

Prerequisites & Limitations

Prerequisites:

  • SAP Integration Suite license and S/4HANA Cloud warehouse integration option.
  • Compatible EWM version (e.g., EWM 9.5 or S/4HANA 2022+ in decentralized mode).
  • Cloud Connector installed and configured, master data aligned.

Limitations:

  • Public Cloud restricts customization—no direct ABAP enhancements.
  • Some advanced EWM features may lack out‑of‑the‑box integration and require custom extensions.
  • Decentralized EWM requires duplicate master data maintenance and may introduce latency at scale.

References

  • SAP Help Portal – Basic Integration with SAP Extended Warehouse Management (SAP EWM): https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/d73c28020d6046c7805db9490d72f41f.html
  • Integrating SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) On-Premise:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implement-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-for-warehouse-management/integrating-sap-extended-warehouse-management-ewm-on-premise-inbound-processes_d2fc88c6-819d-4f55-bfce-78e66e0fae80

  • SAP Help Portal – What’s New in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 1805 (Supply Chain Integration Scope): https://help.sap.com/viewer/product/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1805/en-US
  • SAP API Business Hub – Integration Packages and API Definitions: https://api.sap.com/

entry.title: Part 2 Webinar Entitlement Mgmt: Preview for Scope Item “5SW – Subscription Management for Software"
entry.content:
Join us for this webinar on SAP Entitlement Management, offering Part 2 of the deep dive preview of the planned Scope Item "5SW – Subscription Management for Software" on Tuesday, June 24, 2025, at 4:00 PM CEST / 10:00 AM EST. 

Register now!

This one-hour session is Part 2 of the webinars on scope item 5SW, starting with a short recap of the content of the first webinar held on May 13, 2024, which you can review here,

Part 2 of the webinar will explain and show demos for various subscription change processes which are planned to be supported by the scope item, as well as automated application, tenant and capability decommissioning. 

In addition, we will also have a look into key elements of the system setup for the scope item, for which a detailed setup guide and end to end test script is planned to be made available with the 2508 release. 

We will also give an outlook on the product direction and product vision for "Subscription Management for Software", with SAP Business Data Cloud leveraging license consumption, software usage and adoption data as the basis for customer success, using Analytics Cloud stories, AI Agents, or integration to Gainsight Customer Success. 

Register now and review the detailed agenda below!

More information on EMS - see my Summary Blog on SAP Entitlement Management 

Customer Webinar Part 2 – SAP Entitlement Management:
Deep Dive Preview for Scope Item “5SW – Subscription Management for Software”

Date: Tuesday, June 24, 2025
Time: 4:00 PM Central European Summer Time / 10:00 AM EST
Duration: 1 hour

Agenda:

  • Introduction & Overview of Scope Item 5SW
    • Purpose & business value of Subscription Management for Software
    • Key capabilities: End-to-end integration and automation from sales to fulfillment/provisioning, billing, and revenue recognition
  • Live Demo: Subscription Management in Action – Subscription Change Processes, System Setup for Automated Provisioning, Business Data Cloud and AI Providing Insights for Customer Success Processes
    • Summary of demo flow in first session on May 13, 2025 (review here)
    • Demo of different change processes, e.g.
      • Change of licensed quantity and subscription end date
      • Add-on contract items for more users, more documents, additional test tenants, free trial licenses
      • Contract replacement moving to higher license edition without tenant disruption
      • End of subscription and entitlement license and tenant de-commissioning
    • Key elements of the system setup to achieve automated application, tenant, license metric, and application capability provisioning and de-commissioning
    • SAP Business Data Cloud – leverage license consumption, software usage and adoption data as the basis for customer success, using Analytics Cloud stories, AI Agents, or integration to Gainsight Customer Success
  • Summary, Q&A

entry.title: 📢 Important Update: New Support Components for SAP Central Business Configuration
entry.content:
We’ve introduced a new and improved list of components for customers to select when creating support tickets for the SAP Central Business Configuration  team. This change is designed to make your support experience more efficient, accurate, and aligned with the evolving structure of SAP Central Business Configuration services.

🧭 Why This Change?

As SAP Central Business Configuration continues to grow and evolve, so do the services and technologies we support. The previous component structure no longer reflected the full scope of our offerings, which sometimes led to delays in routing tickets to the right experts.

With the new component list, we’ve:

  • Introduced more granular and descriptive components to better reflect specific areas of support.
  • Aligned component names with current CBC architecture and terminology.
  • Improved ticket routing to ensure faster and more accurate support.

If you're unsure which component to select, our support portal includes updated descriptions and guidance. You can also reach out to our support team for assistance and any new components made available will be added to our KBA 3594047 - SAP Central Business Configuration new component list.

We appreciate your cooperation and look forward to continuing to support your success with SAP Central Business Configuration.

Thanks,

Kelly & Costi Oprisor

entry.title: Parallel universes: the dual nature of UX and AI among SAP customers at Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
Sapphire 2025 was interesting and revealing. In the keynotes, theater sessions, and ASUG breakouts, SAP experts and executives talked about the latest innovations around artificial intelligence (AI), data, and user experience (UX). The customer questions and survey responses, however, revealed a parallel universe. Many organizations are on such old systems that they are not able to consume SAP’s cool new functionality. In this post, I will discuss some of my observations, based on conversations with customers and partners, and share some polling I did in one of my sessions on the intersection between SAP Fiori and AI. I will also include links to several slide decks, recorded demos, and on-demand sessions where you can dive deeper into UX and AI topics.

Customers are starting to use AI, but still behind on SAP Fiori UX adoption

In ERP1612, “Enhancing User Experience with SAP Fiori and AI”, I asked the audience of around 120 people how they are using AI in their companies. 96 people responded.

 

The 56% percent of the people who are using AI in some way is a big jump from the 37% who responded at ASUG Tech Connect last fall. The main topic in both sessions was SAP Fiori, so the audience profile should be somewhat similar, although the Tech Connect attendees might be more technical as opposed to the business focus at Sapphire.

View the slides from ERP1612.

https://www.slideshare.net/slideshow/embed_code/key/1T0gHqRT6X9ddr?hostedIn=slideshare&page=upload

The central theme was SAP Fiori, so it made sense to level set on where people are on their adoption journey. My expectations were high since SAP Fiori has been available since 2013. Since then, our team has spent many hours talking to 1000s of customers and partners about building SAP Fiori apps and how to streamline the process using our tools and technologies such as SAP Fiori elements, SAP Fiori tools, and new AI accelerators.

But, the reality was very different. Only 9 of 109 respondents are using SAP Fiori across their organizations. 43% are still in the planning stage.

 

 

To help customers move to SAP Business Suite, we had many sessions. One that’s available on demand is “Embark on a successful transformation journey to SAP Business Suite,” featuring Maura Hameroff, Priyadarsana Panda, and Soeren Ruder.

Arpan Shah describes how SAP Business Suite drives transformational value through the suite’s strength and software-as-a-service qualities with integrated processes, data, and AI in the on-demand session “Leverage the benefits of SAP Business Suite in your cloud strategy.”

There were some nice demos of the latest SAP user experience in “SAP's user experience strategy to achieve better outcomes” by Arin Bhowmik and Emil Voutta.

Tom Reiss also had some great sessions on SAP Fiori innovations with nice demos, but unfortunately, they were not recorded.

AI in SAP Cloud ERP wowed the crowd

In Chris Hoffmann’s session, BAI1362, which I heard several people say was the best talk of the entire Sapphire, he showed several key innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Here are the slides and demos from his presentation:

  • Slides in PDF format
  • Insights card creation with Joule
  • AI-assisted easy filter
  • AI-assisted smart summarization
  • AI-assisted error explanation
  • AI-assisted smart solution for situations in My Home
  • SAP Fiori elements Project Accelerator

Several executives from SAP and our customers discussed how AI is transforming business. See their session “Harness the benefits of data and AI in ERP and drive innovation” on demand.

For a bigger picture view on where these AI innovations fit into the broader SAP Business Suite, watch “Transform your enterprise with the power of SAP Business Suite.”

SAP Sapphire is a great place for discovery

The new (to SAP) harmonized product operating model focuses on discovery. Interacting with customers to understand their needs plays a big role for product managers and everyone involved in the development process. Sapphire and the after-hours events provide a great venue for conversations with customers about the problems they want to solve.

Since I’m known for my interest in user experience topics, most of my meetings and questions in my sessions focused on UX and adjacent topics. One of my goals was to gather feedback on what we show and address questions about what people need. While people seemed to like the new AI functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that Chris and I showed in our respective sessions, the most common question was when it is coming to private cloud (planned) and on-prem (not planned).

There were several sessions on organizational change management, which seemed to be one of the main reasons that people have not migrated off the SAP GUI, even though they like to complain about it. Some of the main takeaways from the OCM panel were:

  • Prioritize people not technology
  • Change management takes a toll on people – prep for the after.
  • Don’t lose sight of the goal and decommission the old technology. Make sure to listen and be agile.
  • The journey will be difficult, acknowledge this and over-communicate.

I really enjoyed Paul Saunders’ entertaining and informative sessions on “Leveraging dark data for enhanced decision-making” and “Antifragile enterprises: How true resilience comes from embracing change.” These too were not recorded.

Too much content; too little time

Content overload was a common theme I heard from many of the people I spoke with at Sapphire. There were so many sessions crammed into the compressed 2-day format (compared to three days in years past).

BTP remained a hot topic, with many people looking to understand the best way to obtain value.

Discover SAP Business Technology Platform and its value for your business is a good overview on this.

Another good BTP session is “Unleash the power of your applications, data, and AI with SAP BTP.”

AI everywhere – separating reality from hype

AI was top of mind for everyone as it played such a major role in the keynotes. The most common issues that people raised with me were:

  • Concerns about privacy of their data
  • How much of the capability is real and can the results be trusted?
  • Availability of AI functionality outside of SAP Cloud ERP and the associated costs

SAP Sapphire is all about the people

It was great to meet several colleagues in person for the first time and to connect with those that I see only a few times each year.

If we did not meet at Sapphire and you are interested in joining the conversation about SAP Fiori application development and how AI makes it better, faster, and cheaper, please join our monthly SAP Fiori development roundtable group or sign up for our bi-monthly newsletter.

For the UX Foundation team, Peter Spielvogel.
